content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real defects in the same-bead duplicate-creation checks. Amendment 1 correctly observes that RunStatus::Running falls through in reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation, so a Running run.json with lost task-runs.ndjson lineage will let a shadow project be created when a different project_id is passed — violating the fail-closed policy for orphaned/inconsistent bead state. Amendment 2 correctly observes that find_existing_bead_project returns only one project (preferring the default) even though the new 'create after terminal' behavior makes multiple same-bead projects a normal state, which leads to two concrete wrong outcomes: a Completed default can mask a Failed/NotStarted retry that should have blocked creation, and a set of purely-terminal retries without a default throws a generic 'multiple projects' error; the milestone adoption path inherits the same bug. Both are credible, fixable bugs with concrete remediation and test coverage proposals.

## Votes

- `fr-9-71241a55`: Accept
  The fall-through on RunStatus::Running in reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation (src/cli/project.rs:650-666) is only guarded by reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation, which reads task-runs.ndjson via active_task_runs_for_bead. If the lineage row is missing (genuine FS corruption, partial repair, manual edit), the active-runs check returns an empty slice and the Running branch currently does nothing, so passing a different --project-id mints a second project for an ostensibly still-running bead. That contradicts the bead-path fail-closed posture already applied to NotStarted/Failed/Paused and to the unreadable-snapshot branch just above. Treating Running + empty lineage as a repairable error with remediation guidance, plus a CLI test for that exact state, is a proportional and correct fix.
- `fr-9-aa574557`: Accept
  find_existing_bead_project (src/cli/project.rs:677-735) collects all matching task_source projects but only ever returns one — the default if present, otherwise the sole match, otherwise a blanket 'multiple projects already reference bead' error. Now that terminal-state retries are allowed, 'Completed default + Failed retry' is reachable: the helper returns the Completed default, reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation falls through on Completed, and a third project gets created even though the Failed retry should have produced a 'use resume' error. 'No default + multiple terminal retries' is also reachable and produces a misleading generic error instead of either allowing creation or pointing at a resumable attempt. Because src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1172 calls the same helper, controller adoption can grab the wrong project too. Replacing the single-project lookup with a scan that prefers non-terminal states, and only allowing creation when every matching project is terminal, is the correct fix, and the proposed test coverage ('completed default + failed retry', 'multiple terminal retries') targets the exact regressions.
